topic: SAA western cell drift >=0.3 deg/month — c2 verification of c1 patches (5 holes: moderate cognitive-error, minor dead URL, minor 2-month caveat, minor passim citation, minor 0.282 deg/yr attribution)
change_summary: Decider commit 5ab70a1 patched 5 c1 holes on PRED-073 detail_reasoning and sources_cited.
our_argument_summary: PRED-073 (SAA western-cell drift >=0.3 deg April->May 2026) remains pending (window closes 2026-05-31; CHAOS-7 May data early June). The dome's ~12x-over-standard-rate framing is unchanged. Patches landed: cognitive-error speculation removed from detail_reasoning, dead NGDC URL replaced with NCEI canonical, 2-month interval caveat added (both readings <0.1 deg threshold), passim citation replaced with PRED-072 and PRED-093 specifics, 0.282 deg/yr attribution rewritten as "derived from CHAOS-7 SV coefficients evaluated... over 2015-2025". One new minor introduced by patch: PRED-072 cross-reference text says "assessed not-demonstrated" but PRED-072 actual our_verdict is "standard_physics" (data/predictions.json:PRED-072.our_verdict).

per_hole_verification.c1_description: Cognitive-error speculation in detail_reasoning ("possibly by treating the annual rate as a monthly rate during prediction authoring").
per_hole_verification.patch_evidence: detail_reasoning no longer contains the speculative "possibly by treating" clause. Verified via search of data/predictions.json:PRED-073.detail_reasoning at HEAD. However, similar speculation has migrated into kernel_of_truth: "A possible authoring error: confusing the annual rate (~0.28 deg/year) with a monthly rate (~0.3 deg/month)." Same cognitive-state speculation, different field.
per_hole_verification.assessment: Hole 0 closed in the field the c1 review specifically targeted (detail_reasoning). The migration into kernel_of_truth is a scope-judgement call: the c1 recommendation read "Strike or rewrite the sentence in detail_reasoning" without specifying that the principle applies to all fields. The new kernel_of_truth wording is also slightly softer ("a possible authoring error" vs the c1's more direct "possibly by treating"). Flagging as a low-severity follow-up only, not blocking.

per_hole_verification.patch_evidence: sources_cited[2] now reads "IGRF-13 secular variation tables (https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-geomagnetic-reference-field)". Matches the c1 recommendation exactly. URL form is well-known NCEI canonical (live-verification not performed this cycle but URL pattern is standard).

per_hole_verification.c1_description: 2-month interval ambiguity not acknowledged (April->May reading could be 30d or 60d).
per_hole_verification.patch_evidence: detail_reasoning paragraph 3 now reads "This conclusion holds under either reading of the test window: over a 1-month (30-day) interval the expected drift is ~0.024 deg; over a 2-month (60-day) April-May reading it is ~0.047 deg. Both are less than half the dome's own <0.1 deg falsification boundary, so the structural finding is robust to interval interpretation." Closes the defender-escape-via-interval-reinterpretation route exactly as c1 recommended.

Replaced with specific PREDs: "Compare to PRED-072 (assessed not-demonstrated, restates a TTB fluctuation magnitude inside the standard-physics tolerance) and PRED-093 (Schumann resonance during G3 storm, threshold set inside the historical SR storm-response band) — both exemplify the pattern..." — the structure matches c1's recommendation ("Replace with 2-3 specific PRED-IDs"). However PRED-072's actual our_verdict is "standard_physics" not "not-demonstrated" (data/predictions.json:PRED-072.our_verdict). The patcher's own c1 recommendation used the phrase "assessed standard_physics" — the integration introduced a small factual slip.
per_hole_verification.assessment: Patch addressed the c1 hole but introduced a NEW minor: cross-reference verdict mismatch. See holes_found[0] below.

per_hole_verification.c1_description: 0.282 deg/yr attribution to Finlay 2020 without the paper actually quoting that specific value.
per_hole_verification.patch_evidence: detail_reasoning paragraph 1 now reads "0.282 +/- 0.030 deg/year derived from CHAOS-7 secular-variation coefficients evaluated at the western-cell longitude over 2015-2025 (Finlay et al. 2020 ... ; Pavon-Carrasco & De Santis 2016 SAA baseline)." Matches c1 option (b) exactly: "re-attribute as derived from CHAOS-7 SV coefficients evaluated".

holes_found.description: PRED-072 CROSS-REFERENCE VERDICT MISMATCH (newly introduced by c1->c2 patch on hole 3). The patched text in detail_reasoning paragraph 3 reads: "Compare to PRED-072 (assessed not-demonstrated, restates a TTB fluctuation magnitude inside the standard-physics tolerance)..." — but data/predictions.json:PRED-072.our_verdict is currently "standard_physics", not "not-demonstrated". This is a single-character-class label error (the patcher's own c1 recommendation used the correct phrase "assessed standard_physics"). A reader cross-checking PRED-072 will find the verdict label mismatch and flag it. Self-inconsistency at WIN-019 c5/c6 scale is what made this review pipeline catch the original 0.4%/0.2% bug — same class of error.
holes_found.recommendation: Two equivalent one-line edits: (a) change "assessed not-demonstrated" to "assessed standard_physics" in PRED-073.detail_reasoning paragraph 3; OR (b) leave "not-demonstrated" if the operator wants to keep that framing and instead update PRED-072.our_verdict accordingly (but PRED-072 already has standard_physics, and the framing "restates a known TTB fluctuation magnitude inside the standard-physics tolerance" is the canonical standard_physics description — so (a) is the natural fix).

holes_found.description: KERNEL_OF_TRUTH RESIDUAL of c1 Hole 0. The kernel_of_truth field still contains: "A possible authoring error: confusing the annual rate (~0.28 deg/year) with a monthly rate (~0.3 deg/month)." This is similar cognitive-state speculation to what was removed from detail_reasoning per c1 hole 0. The c1 recommendation was field-specific ("in detail_reasoning") and the patcher honored that scope, so this is not strictly a patch defect — but the same speculation in a different field is still quote-mineable by a defender. The c1 hole 0 framing was "this is not the move that gives the verification credibility — it speculates about the author's cognitive state without evidence."
holes_found.recommendation: Optional follow-up: replace kernel_of_truth's "A possible authoring error" sentence with a neutral observational equivalent, e.g.: "The numerical mismatch could equally reflect an aetheric-flux claim distinct from the standard secular variation rate or a labeling discrepancy between annual and monthly cadence — the dome's public text does not disambiguate." Severity is minor and decider may legitimately defer or wontfix (the c1 recommendation did not extend to this field).

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c1 — dome correctly identifies the SAA western cell as drifting westward, direction matches IGRF-13/CHAOS-7 (data/predictions.json:PRED-073.kernel_of_truth). Aetheric-return-flow mechanism is the right order of magnitude (0.2-0.5 deg/year) for core-flow theory. The failure mode is purely numerical: dome committed to ~12x faster rate than measured.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Order-of-magnitude rate mismatch (3.65 deg/year annualised vs 0.282 deg/year IGRF-13 baseline) puts the prediction outside both standard secular variation and the CHAOS-7 noise band. Dome's own falsification rule (<0.1 deg/month) is triggered by the consensus null hypothesis itself (data/predictions.json:PRED-073.detail_reasoning).

deeper_analysis: Advocate-mode check on the patched text: (1) Defender attack via interval re-reading is now closed (paragraph 3 explicitly addresses both 1-month and 2-month readings). (2) Defender attack via secular-variation rate dispute: the patched attribution "derived from CHAOS-7 SV coefficients" is honest about derivation chain and harder to attack than the c1 "Finlay 2020" attribution. (3) Defender attack via cross-reference label: a defender pointing at "PRED-072 not-demonstrated" vs actual standard_physics could claim our review has factual errors — this is the new minor flagged above. (4) Defender attack on the 0.024 deg arithmetic (0.282 / 12 ~= 0.024) is correct; no opening there. Cross-WIN consistency: the cited PRED-072 / PRED-093 examples are real entries and broadly match the framing (PRED-072 IS a standard-physics-tolerance restate; PRED-093 IS a threshold inside historical SR storm-response band). Quantitative verification: 3.65 / 0.282 = 12.94 (matches "~12-13x"); 0.024 deg over 30 days = 0.024 / (0.282/365 * 30) = 1.04 (matches "order of magnitude below 0.1 deg"). Math is sound.

citations_verified: Finlay et al. 2020 DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (URL form verified)
citations_unchecked: CSES proton-flux 2025 DOI 10.1007/s11430-025-1672-2 (URL form plausible but not live-checked this cycle)
Pavon-Carrasco & De Santis 2016 (cited as baseline, no DOI in text)

summary_for_decider: BATCH VERIFICATION qid=399 (PRED-073 c2, batch position 2). 4 of 5 c1 holes patched cleanly: dead URL (closed), 2-month caveat (closed), passim citation (replaced with PRED-072/PRED-093 — but with a new minor verdict-label error, see hole 0), 0.282 deg/yr attribution (closed). 1 of 5 partially patched: cognitive-error speculation removed from detail_reasoning (c1 target) but a softer variant remains in kernel_of_truth (hole 1 follow-up, minor). Two new minor holes flagged — both single-line edits, no verdict change. Verdict remains pending — CHAOS-7 May 2026 data arrives early June 2026, the structural conclusion is robust under both 1-month and 2-month interval readings.
